notes: SL-2 per IEC 62443-3-3: protection against intentional violation by skilled attackers using standard means. Applicable because rust-LIN may be integrated into control systems communicating over shared LIN bus segments.

practices.title: Security management
practices.notes: SECURITY.md, INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md, responsible disclosure policy documented

practices.title: Identification of responsibilities
practices.notes: Single maintainer identified in SECURITY.md with noreply contact

practices.title: Security training
practices.notes: SECURITY.md and CODING_STANDARD.md document secure coding guidelines; formal training record not maintained at this scale

practices.title: Security requirements derivation
practices.notes: REQ-SEC-001..008 in requirements.json, derived from TARA threat scenarios TARA-001..012

practices.title: Security requirements for software
practices.notes: All REQ-SEC requirements allocated to source modules with //fusa:req annotations; traced in .fusa-reqs.json

practices.title: Secure design principles
practices.notes: No unsafe code; all input validated at RELAY boundary; least-privilege — library has no network or file I/O itself

practices.title: Secure design review
practices.notes: ARCHITECTURE.md documents all data flows; BOUNDARY_DIAGRAM.md defines trust boundaries; FMEA and TARA reviewed against design

practices.title: Secure implementation
practices.notes: cargo clippy -D warnings; no unsafe blocks; no dynamic code execution; all strings are owner-allocated (Vec/String)

practices.title: Security testing during implementation
practices.notes: REQ-SEC-001..008 each have ≥1 //fusa:sec-test annotation; TARA countermeasures exercised by integration_test.rs

practices.title: Security verification and validation
practices.notes: cargo audit in CI; security unit tests in integration_test.rs; rsfusa cyber --dir . in safety CI job

practices.title: Fuzz and negative testing
practices.notes: LDF parser fuzz-resistance tested with empty/malformed input (REQ-LDF-014, REQ-SEC-004); structured fuzzing via cargo-fuzz is planned (ROADMAP.md)

practices.title: Defect management
practices.notes: GitHub Issues; INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md; cargo audit automated

practices.title: Security defect tracking
practices.notes: Security issues tracked in GitHub Issues with 'security' label; CVE assignment via maintainer disclosure process

practices.title: Security update management
practices.notes: Dependabot; cargo audit on every CI run; patch release process in INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md

practices.title: Patch and update communication
practices.notes: GitHub Releases include security advisory notes; SECURITY.md defines disclosure timeline (90 days)

threats.description: Malformed LIN frame injected via RELAY adapter
threats.mitigation: from_message validates protocol, id range, and data via validate_frame
validate_frame rejects data.len() > 8; Rust Vec is bounds-safe
calc_checksum uses u16 accumulator with explicit carry-around; no overflow possible

threats.description: Race condition in concurrent bus access
threats.mitigation: All shared state protected by tokio::sync::Mutex or atomic operations
cargo audit runs on every CI build; Cargo.lock committed

threats.description: E2E CRC forgery via crafted protected payload
threats.mitigation: CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE detects unintentional corruption; full MAC is SEOOC assumption (REQ-SEOOC-008)
threats.status: accepted — residual risk acknowledged in TARA-006

threats.description: Sequence counter replay attack via replayed protect()ed message
LDF injection — hostile LDF file with out-of-range frame IDs
threats.mitigation: parse() never panics (REQ-LDF-014); ID validation at MasterNode level; REQ-SEC-004

threats.description: Cross-protocol message routing — non-LIN message accepted as LIN
threats.mitigation: from_message checks protocol == Protocol::Lin; REQ-ADAPT-002, REQ-SEC-002

threats.description: Denial of service via high-frequency master frame sends
threats.mitigation: rate_limit_per_sec subscriber option; back-pressure is non-blocking; REQ-SEC-007

threats.description: Unsafe code introduction enabling memory safety violations
threats.mitigation: rsfusa lint checks for unsafe blocks; REQ-SEC-006; cargo clippy -D warnings
